title: • Tied to a specific moment in the semester (ex: Week 8).
content:
• Tied to a specific moment in the semester (ex: Week 8).
• Players must offer a sacrifice (XP, a rare item, or a personal weakness).
• The ritual alters one major trait:
• Faction switch.
• Alignment shift.
• Symbol/Icon change (which grants a new passive effect).

✔ Example Ritual Sacrifices:
• A player can burn an item of great value to erase a faction betrayal.
• A student may offer XP to the altar in exchange for rewriting their story arc.
• A player may accept a new debuff to access a different archetype transformation.

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Prevents students from constantly switching major traits.
✔ Adds a powerful narrative moment with risk and consequence.
✔ Ties into mythological themes of sacrifice, fate, and destiny.

📜 3. The Fate Wheel is a Public Spectacle
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 518 (chunk 518/555)
